title: sterigmostemum sulphureum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Sterigmostemum sulphureum subsp. glandulosum is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of daisy. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Sterigmostemum sulphureum subsp. glandulosum is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically in the form of creams or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
